name: Massachusetts Dietitian Registration
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Massachusetts - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Registration of Dietitians and Nutritionists

address.description: Board of Registration of Dietitians and Nutritionists, 1000 Washington Street, Suite 710, Boston, MA 02118-6100

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcript (Sealed)
requiredDocuments.description: Sealed by the registrar, verifying the highest degree earned. If your degree is not in a directly related field, include a completed 'Course Summary Form' with transcripts.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Card or BCNS Letter
requiredDocuments.description: A copy of your current CDR registration card (for RDs) or an official sealed letter from BCNS confirming passage (for CNS applicants).

requiredDocuments.name: Verification of Supervised Experience
requiredDocuments.description: A 'Verification by Supervisor' form signed by your supervising dietitian, plus your professional resume.

requiredDocuments.name: CORI Acknowledgment Form
requiredDocuments.description: Criminal Offender Record Information acknowledgment is required as part of the application.

requiredDocuments.name: License Verification from Other States
requiredDocuments.description: If you hold or have held licenses in other jurisdictions, a certificate of standing must be obtained from each.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Massachusetts dietitian/nutritionist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The combined application and initial license fee is $196, payable to the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. The biennial renewal fee is also $196.
The Board of Registration of Dietitians and Nutritionists, which operates under the Massachusetts Department of Public Health (DPH), is the regulatory body.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for Massachusetts dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Licensees must complete a minimum of 30 units (each unit = 50 minutes of organized learning) of continuing education in the field of dietetics and nutrition during each two-year renewal cycle. The Board may conduct random audits.

mainEntity.name: Does Massachusetts have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Massachusetts. To work in Massachusetts, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Massachusetts?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Massachusetts while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
